name: Plan → Test → Code → Review
description: TDD workflow — plan first, write tests, then implement, then review

stages.name: Plan
stages.description: Analyze the task and create an implementation plan
stages.systemPromptAppend: You are in the PLANNING stage. Analyze the task, explore the codebase, and create a detailed implementation plan. Identify what tests need to be written. Do NOT make any code changes yet. When your plan is complete, summarize it and say "Planning complete."

stages.name: Test
stages.systemPromptAppend: You are in the TESTING stage (TDD red phase). Write tests FIRST based on the plan. These tests should define the expected behavior and FAIL initially — that is correct. Do not write implementation code yet. When tests are written, say "Tests written."

stages.name: Code
stages.description: Implement to make tests pass (TDD green phase)
stages.systemPromptAppend: You are in the CODING stage (TDD green phase). Implement the minimum code needed to make the tests pass. Follow the project's coding conventions. Run tests to verify they pass. When all tests pass, say "Implementation complete."

stages.name: Review
stages.systemPromptAppend:
You are in the REVIEW stage (TDD refactor phase). Review all changes. Refactor for clarity without changing behavior. Check for:
- Code quality and style consistency
- Missing error handling
- Security issues
- Test coverage adequacy
Run tests after any refactoring. When satisfied, say "Review complete."
